tools.name: add_comment
tools.description: Add a Trados Studio comment to a segment (same as the editor's Add Comment command) – e.g. to flag a source issue for the client or leave a review note. Becomes part of the document's unsaved changes. Only add comments the user asked for or agreed to.

severity.description: Severity: Low (informational, default), Medium (warning), or High (error).

tools.name: add_term
tools.description: Add a source/target term pair to the user's configured Supervertaler Write termbases (same as Supervertaler's quick-add). Reports which termbases received the term, or an error if it already exists. NOTE: Trados's own project termbases (.ttb / MultiTerm) are READ-ONLY for you – if the user wants a term added there, tell them to add it in Trados itself. Only use when the user asked for a term to be added, or explicitly agreed to your suggestion to add one.

tools.name: check_numbers
tools.description: QA check: find translated segments where the numbers in source and target don't match (digits compared with thousand/decimal separators normalised, so 1.234,56 matches 1,234.56). Returns each mismatch with the segment's numbers listed. Only translated segments are checked. Chain with update_segments after the user approves fixes.

tools.name: check_tags
tools.description: QA check: find translated segments whose inline tag count differs between source and target (missing or extra formatting/placeholder tags). A difference is not always an error – formatting can legitimately differ – so present findings for review rather than auto-fixing.

tools.name: check_terminology
tools.description: QA check: find termbase terms that appear in source segments whose targets don't use the expected translation (or any synonym). Covers Supervertaler termbases AND the Trados project's own termbases (.ttb/MultiTerm). Results are GROUPED PER TERM, most-affected first: each group has the term, its termbase, the expected translations, how many segments are affected, and sample segment ids. A term affecting many segments usually means the project consistently uses a different translation than the termbase – help the user decide which is right (update_segments to align the project, or add_term/edit the termbase) rather than auto-fixing. Substring-based, so inflected target forms can be false positives.

tools.name: export_target
tools.description: Start Trados Studio's Generate Target Translations batch task: writes the translated target files from the last-saved state into the project's target-language folder. Runs in the BACKGROUND and returns a jobId immediately; poll get_task_status with that jobId for completion. In Studio, right-click a file and choose Open Target, or open the project folder, to find the generated files.

tools.name: analyze
tools.description: Start Trados Studio's Analyse Files batch task on the open project. Computes the leverage breakdown (perfect/in-context-exact/exact/fuzzy/new/repetition words and segments) and writes it into the project, which is what get_project_statistics reads back. Runs in the BACKGROUND and returns a jobId immediately – analysis can take several minutes on a large project. Poll get_task_status with that jobId until it reports 'done', then call get_project_statistics to read the bands. Runs against the LAST SAVED state, so ask the user to save first.

tools.name: get_task_status
tools.description: Check a background batch task started by analyze, pretranslate, update_tm, or export_target. Pass the jobId that tool returned. Returns status ('running', 'done', or 'failed'), elapsed seconds, any task messages (e.g. pre-translate match counts), and a note. Poll this every 20-30 seconds until status is no longer 'running'. For analyze, once it's 'done', call get_project_statistics to read the leverage bands.

jobId.description: The jobId returned by the batch-task tool (analyze/pretranslate/update_tm/export_target).

tools.name: find_and_replace
tools.description: Find and replace text in the TARGET (translation) side of segments across the open Trados document. Options: caseSensitive, wholeWord, regex, and filters by file (merged documents) or confirmation status. STRONGLY RECOMMENDED workflow: first call with dryRun=true to preview which segments would change (returns before/after), show the user, then call again with dryRun=false to apply. Matches that straddle inline tags are skipped for safety and reported. Locked segments are skipped. Changes land in the open document; the user saves in Studio. Only replace when the user asked for it.

find.description: The text (or regex pattern) to find in target segments.

replace.description: The replacement text (empty string deletes the found text).

dryRun.description: Preview only – report what would change without writing. Do this first for anything non-trivial.

wholeWord.description: Match whole words only. Default false.

regex.description: Treat 'find' as a .NET regular expression (and 'replace' may use $1 etc.). Default false.

file.description: Restrict to one file of a merged document (id or partial name from get_files). Omit for all.

status.description: Restrict to segments with this confirmation status (e.g. Draft, Translated). Omit for all.

tools.name: find_inconsistencies
tools.description: Find translation inconsistencies in the document open in the Trados editor: repeated source segments (compared tag-stripped) whose translations differ. Returns each inconsistent group with its source text and every occurrence (segment id, target, status, file). Pair with update_segments to align occurrences after the user chooses the preferred translation.

limit.description: Maximum number of inconsistent groups to return (default 50).

tools.name: get_active_project
tools.description: Get the Trados Studio project currently open in the editor: project name, source and target language, the document/file being edited, and progress statistics (segment counts per confirmation status, word counts). Call this first to orient yourself.

tools.name: get_active_segment
tools.description: Get the segment the translator is working on right now, with its surrounding segments, TM matches and termbase hits for it, and project metadata. Use this when the user says 'this segment' or asks about what they are currently editing.

tools.name: get_comments
tools.description: Read the Trados comments on a segment (author, date, severity, text), in a stable order whose index update_comment uses. Call before updating a comment.

tools.name: get_files
tools.description: List the files of the document open in the Trados editor: file id, name, segment count, and which one is active. Normal documents have one file; merged documents have several. Use the names/ids with get_segments' 'file' filter to work per file.

tools.name: get_project_statistics
tools.description: Get the project's analysis statistics (per language direction: perfect/exact/fuzzy/new/repetition words and segments) and per-file confirmation statistics (not started, draft, translated, approved, signed off) from the project file on disk. Use this for questions about word counts, progress, or how much work is left. Defaults to the project open in the editor.

projectName.description: Project name. Omit to use the project open in the Trados editor.

tools.name: get_segments
tools.description: List segments of the document open in the Trados editor. Returns segment id, source text, target text, confirmation status (e.g. Unspecified/Draft/Translated), locked flag, and 'number' – the segment number the user sees in Studio's grid. ALWAYS cite that number (plus the file name in merged documents, where numbers restart per file) when referring to segments in conversation – never invent numbers. Inline formatting appears as tags like … or … – preserve them in any translation you propose. Use the filters to fetch only what you need instead of the whole document. Results are paged via limit/offset; the response says whether it was truncated.

status.description: Only segments with this confirmation status: Unspecified, Draft, Translated, RejectedTranslation, ApprovedTranslation, RejectedSignOff or ApprovedSignOff. Omit for all.

contains.description: Only segments whose source or target contains this text (case-insensitive). Omit for all.

limit.description: Maximum number of segments to return (default 200).

offset.description: Number of matching segments to skip, for paging (default 0).

file.description: Only segments from this file of a merged multi-file document – a file id or (partial) file name from get_files. Omit for all files.

tools.name: go_to_segment
tools.description: Move Trados Studio's editor to a specific segment, so the user sees the segment you are talking about. Address it by the full id from get_segments, OR by the segment number the user sees in Studio's grid (plus the file name in merged multi-file documents, since numbers restart per file). Use this after flagging or editing a segment.

id.description: Full segment id ("<paragraphUnitId>:<segmentId>") from get_segments. Alternative to number.

number.description: Segment number as displayed in Studio's grid. Alternative to id.

file.description: File name or id (from get_files) – required with 'number' in merged documents.

tools.name: insert_into_active_segment
tools.description: Insert text into the target side of the segment the translator is currently editing in Trados Studio (same as Supervertaler's Apply-to-target button). Replaces the current target content of that one segment. Only use when the user asked you to apply or insert a translation.

text.description: The target-language text to insert. Preserve inline tags from the source where appropriate.

tools.name: list_resources
tools.description: List the translation resources available: the Trados TMs attached to the open project (file-based and GroupShare server TMs), the Supervertaler bridged TMs, and ALL termbases - Supervertaler ones (kind: supervertaler, writable if flagged) and the Trados project's own (kind: trados-ttb or multiterm, always read-only). Useful for orientation before searching, and to answer 'what TMs/termbases am I using?'.

tools.name: lookup_term
tools.description: Look up a term across ALL the user's termbases: Supervertaler termbases plus the Trados termbases attached to the open project (.ttb in Studio 2026, MultiTerm .sdltb in Studio 2024 – hits from those are marked [Trados project termbase]). Exact match first, substring fallback. The user's termbase is authoritative for terminology – always follow it over your own preference.

term.description: The term to look up (source or target language).

tools.name: pretranslate
tools.description: Start Trados Studio's Pre-translate Files batch task: fills untranslated segments with their TM matches (typically exact/context matches). Runs in the BACKGROUND and returns a jobId immediately (it can take a while); poll get_task_status with that jobId to see when it finishes and to read its match-count messages. WRITES into the document from the LAST SAVED state, so ask the user to save first; if the document is open in the editor they may need to close and reopen it to see the results (and if the task fails because the file is open, tell them to close the document and retry).

tools.name: run_verification
tools.description: Run Trados Studio's OWN QA verification (Verify Files / F8 – QA Checker 3.0, tag and term verifiers: punctuation, brackets, repeated words, spelling, regex rules, length checks, etc.) and return the findings, each with file, segment number, the full segment 'id' (usable with go_to_segment / add_comment / update_segments), severity, origin (the QA rule), and message. This catches things the check_* tools don't (punctuation, spelling, regex) and complements them. Already-ignored messages are excluded. IMPORTANT: it reads the LAST SAVED state of the files – if the user made edits (including ones you applied with update_segments), tell them to save in Studio first, then run again. Triage each finding against the source before proposing fixes; some are false positives.

tools.name: search_studio_tm
tools.description: Concordance-search the native Trados translation memories attached to the open project – the .sdltm files and GroupShare server TMs the user actually translates against (the same TMs as Supervertaler's SuperSearch). Returns previous source/target pairs with match scores and the TM name. This is usually the right tool for "how did I translate this before?"; search_tm only covers the separate Supervertaler-bridged TMs.

text.description: The text to search for (a word or phrase from the segment).

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default 10).

tools.name: search_tm
tools.description: Search the user's *Supervertaler* translation memories (the ones bridged from Supervertaler Workbench) for matches to a text. For the native Trados TMs attached to the project – which is what most users work with – use search_studio_tm instead, or call both. Returns previous translations with scores; use before proposing a translation to ground it in the user's past work.

text.description: The text to find TM matches for (typically one segment or sentence).

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to return (default 5).

tools.name: update_comment
tools.description: Replace the text of an existing Trados comment on a segment – e.g. when a fix you applied makes the old comment text outdated. Address the comment by the index from get_comments (call it first). Only update comments the user asked you to change.

tools.name: update_segments
tools.description: Write translations and/or set confirmation statuses for segments in the document open in Trados Studio, addressed by the ids returned by get_segments. Target text may reuse the inline tag markers from the source (…, …) – preserve them. A target write without an explicit status is set to Draft automatically so the user can review your work in Studio. Locked segments are refused. Maximum 200 updates per call – page larger jobs. Only use when the user asked you to change segments, and afterwards tell them exactly what you changed. Changes land in the open document; the user still needs to save it in Studio.

updates.description: Segments to update. Each item: id (required, from get_segments), target (optional new target text), status (optional: Unspecified, Draft, Translated, RejectedTranslation, ApprovedTranslation, RejectedSignOff, ApprovedSignOff). Provide target, status, or both per item.

id.description: Segment id as returned by get_segments ("<paragraphUnitId>:<segmentId>").

target.description: New target text. Omit to change only the status. Preserve inline tag markers from the source.

status.description: Confirmation status to set: Unspecified, Draft, Translated, RejectedTranslation, ApprovedTranslation, RejectedSignOff or ApprovedSignOff. Omitted with a target write = Draft.

tools.name: update_tm
tools.description: Start Trados Studio's Update Main Translation Memories batch task: writes confirmed segments from the last-saved state into the project's main TM(s). Runs in the BACKGROUND and returns a jobId immediately; poll get_task_status with that jobId for completion. If the user just confirmed segments in the editor, tell them to save first and run this again to include them.

tools.name: list_prompts
tools.description: List the prompts in the user's Supervertaler prompt library (Markdown files, shared with the Supervertaler Workbench). Returns each prompt's name, description, folder/category, relativePath (pass it to get_prompt or save_prompt), and flags (isDefault=built-in, isQuickLauncher=appears in the editor right-click menu, isReadOnly). Use this first to see what prompts exist before reading or improving one.

category.description: Optional: only return prompts whose folder/category contains this text (e.g. "Translate", "QuickLauncher", "Proofread").

query.description: Optional: only return prompts whose name or description contains this text.

tools.name: get_prompt
tools.description: Get the full text and metadata of one prompt from the Supervertaler prompt library. Identify it by 'path' (the relativePath from list_prompts, preferred) or by exact 'name'. Use this to read a prompt before discussing or improving it.

path.description: The prompt's relativePath from list_prompts (e.g. "Translate/Default/Default Translation Prompt.md"). Preferred, unambiguous.

name.description: Exact prompt name (filename without extension). Used only if 'path' is omitted.

tools.name: save_prompt
tools.description: Create a new prompt, or update an existing one, in the Supervertaler prompt library (shared with the Workbench). To UPDATE, pass the existing prompt's 'path' (relativePath from list_prompts) plus the new 'content'. To CREATE, pass a 'name' and 'content' (and optionally 'category' as the folder, e.g. "Translate" or "QuickLauncher/Default" to make it a right-click menu item). Prompts support placeholders like {{SOURCE_LANGUAGE}}, {{TARGET_LANGUAGE}}, {{SOURCE_SEGMENT}}. Built-in default prompts cannot be overwritten – save your version under a new name. Ideal for saving an improved prompt you refined together with the user.

path.description: relativePath of an existing prompt to UPDATE (from list_prompts). Omit to create a new prompt.

name.description: Name for a NEW prompt (filename, no extension). Required when creating; ignored when 'path' is given.

description.description: Optional one-line description stored in the prompt's frontmatter.

category.description: Optional folder for a NEW prompt, e.g. "Translate", "Proofread", or "QuickLauncher/Default" (right-click menu). Omit for the library root.
